page: Amazon Web Services r6g.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this server features an arm64 architecture utilizing the AWS Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It provides 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a mixture of average and weak performance metrics. In stress-ng testing, the instance achieves average single-core (1519.59) and multi-core (6051.15) CPU performance. Large-block memory bandwidth tests show average results with 70.15 GB/sec for read-only and 34.19 GB/sec for write operations, though small-block cached read-only bandwidth is weaker at 235.93 GB/sec. The server exhibits poor to weak performance in single-threaded tasks, such as bzip3 decompression (7.34 GB/sec) and Geekbench single-core (938.0) and multi-core (3156.0) tests. PassMark database operations score in the average tier at 2654.31, while Redis SET operations register at a weaker 4.73 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6g.xlarge leverages the ARMv8 architecture to deliver a high-density memory profile at a lower resource-to-cost ratio compared to traditional x86 memory-optimized instances. However, workloads requiring intensive single-threaded execution, complex cryptography, or local high-speed storage may face performance tradeoffs due to the lack of local NVMe drives and weaker single-core benchmark scores. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand extreme compute power, such as medium-sized database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose memory-optimized applications.
description: Amazon Web Services r6g.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware. Benchmark results indicate average performance in stress-ng CPU tests and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside average PassMark database operation scores. However, it displays weaker results in single-threaded tasks, bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations. This server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy applications by utilizing energy-efficient ARM architecture. It is best suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and memory-optimized general-purpose applications that do not require high-speed local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6g.xlarge is a memory-optimized arm64 instance with 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, suited for database and caching workloads.

tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance offering 32 GB of RAM and dedicated Graviton cores for database and caching workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Nitro hypervisor with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth

No local storage or GPU accelerators included
